title: Issue with simlib.v, Unsupported: Verilog 1995 gate primitive: tran for Verilator
body:
### Version

0.57

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Issue from this commit
https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/commit/1251e92e3a988b33f2075118071f4454247041d6

1. Using Verilator 
```bash
Verilator 5.039 devel rev v5.038-8-gd89df33fc
```

2. Run folder of simlib.v
```bash
verilator --binary -f verilator.f simlib.v
```

3. verilator.f
```bash

--timing
-j 0
-Wall
-Wno-fatal
--assert
--trace-fst
--trace-structs
--main-top-name "-"

--x-assign unique
--x-initial unique

-Werror-IMPLICIT
-Werror-USERERROR
-Werror-LATCH
```

### Expected Behavior

1. Parse all the Verilog files listed in verilator.f plus simlib.v.
2. Treat simlib as the top module.
3. Generate a C++ simulation model.
4. Link it into a runnable binary.
5. Able tp execute simulation.

### Actual Behavior

```bash
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: simlib.v:3229:6: Unsupported: Verilog 1995 gate primitive: tran
 3229 | tran connect[WIDTH-1:0] (A, B);
      |      ^~~~~~~
                    ... For error description see https://verilator.org/warn/UNSUPPORTED?v=5.039
%Error: Exiting due to 2 error(s)
```
code_blocks:
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: simlib.v:3229:6: Unsupported: Verilog 1995 gate primitive: tran
 3229 | tran connect[WIDTH-1:0] (A, B);
      |      ^~~~~~~
                    ... For error description see https://verilator.org/warn/UNSUPPORTED?v=5.039
%Error: Exiting due to 2 error(s)
